seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Mastra Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Mastra agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Mastra, Context Window Management issues for AI agents arise when workflow steps accumulate excessive state data, memory retrievals inject irrelevant historical context, and tool outputs exceed token limits without truncation. Agents often hallucinate or fail mid-conversation due to context overflow in Mastra's workflow transitions and agent memory systems. These problems are exacerbated by Mastra's flexible state management which doesn't enforce token limits by default.

howItManifests.scenario: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
howItManifests.symptom: Agent receives full conversation history plus unfiltered tool outputs exceeding 128k token limit
howItManifests.impact: LLM returns 400 errors or truncated responses, breaking workflow continuity

howItManifests.symptom: Mastra's agentMemory.retrieve() pulls 50+ previous conversations into current context
howItManifests.impact: Agent confuses user identities and conversation topics across sessions

howItManifests.scenario: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
howItManifests.symptom: Third-party tool responses contain binary data or excessive JSON payloads
howItManifests.impact: Context window overflows during tool result processing in workflow nodes

howItManifests.scenario: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
howItManifests.symptom: Real-time data feeds continuously append to agent context without summarization
howItManifests.impact: Agent fixates on latest data point, ignoring user intent and conversation flow

detection.name: Token Limit Exceeded Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts Mastra workflow transitions to measure context length before LLM calls

detection.name: Memory Injection Validator
detection.description: Validates Mastra agentMemory retrievals for relevance and staleness

detection.name: Tool Output Size Checker
detection.description: Scans Mastra tool call results for oversized payloads before context injection

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Truncator
fixes.description: Automatically summarizes and prioritizes Mastra workflow context to fit token limits

fixes.name: Smart Memory Filter
fixes.description: Filters Mastra agent memory by recency, relevance, and conversation boundaries

fixes.name: Tool Result Compressor
fixes.description: Compresses large Mastra tool outputs with structured summaries

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly into Mastra's middleware pipeline as a zero-config observability layer, automatically detecting context window violations across workflows, agents, and tools. It provides real-time token counting, relevance scoring, and automatic truncation with full audit trails. Vex's Mastra integration captures every context injection point - workflow transitions, memory retrievals, tool results - and applies ML-powered filtering to maintain optimal context hygiene.

The Vex SDK hooks into Mastra's runtime without code changes, providing instant dashboards showing context bloat patterns, top memory offenders, and tool output culprits. Automated remediation rules trigger summarization, filtering, or workflow rerouting when thresholds are hit, ensuring agents stay within token limits 99.9% of the time. Teams get Slack/Teams alerts with one-click fixes and historical trend analysis for capacity planning.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `npm install @vex_dev/sdk` and one line of code to eliminate context window failures forever.
